input.command: cat run.json 2>/dev/null | head -50; echo "---PROMPT---"; ls; echo "---"; cat prompt.txt 2>/dev/null | head -60

content.content:
---PROMPT---
agent.log
precondition.log
prompt.txt
proxy-client.crt
proxy-client.key
proxy.pid
---
The Elasticsearch cluster cannot form and logs show "master not discovered yet".
Restore a healthy cluster and ensure the fix is safe for future restarts.

Constraints:
- No operators or CRDs.
- Use existing resources; do not recreate the cluster.

Goal:
- Cluster is healthy (yellow/green) with 3 nodes and a master elected.
- Bootstrap-only settings are removed after the cluster forms.
- A rolling restart keeps the cluster healthy.

Restore a healthy cluster and ensure the fix is safe for future restarts.

Constraints:
- No operators or CRDs.
- Use existing resources; do not recreate the cluster.

Goal:
- Cluster is healthy (yellow/green) with 3 nodes and a master elected.
- Bootstrap-only settings are removed after the cluster forms.
- A rolling restart keeps the cluster healthy.

apiVersion: v1
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${POD_NAME}
    node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - es-cluster-0.es-cluster
      - es-cluster-1.es-cluster
      - es-cluster-2.es-cluster
    node.store.allow_mmap: false

content.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:

tool_use_result.stdout: {"type": "server", "timestamp": "2026-06-18T13:49:11,925Z", "level": "INFO", "component": "o.e.n.Node", "cluster.name": "es-cluster", "node.name": "es-cluster-0", "message": "node name [es-cluster-0], node ID [YJmPFM-gTkKUdOeL0idUOA], cluster name [es-cluster], roles [master, data, ingest]" }

content.text: Diagnosis confirmed: the `es-config` ConfigMap has `discovery.seed_hosts` but **no `cluster.initial_master_nodes`** — so a brand-new ES 7.x cluster (empty data dirs) can never bootstrap a voting configuration, hence "master not discovered yet". Let me add the bootstrap setting.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/es-config-bootstrap.yaml <<'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${POD_NAME}
    node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - es-cluster-0.es-cluster
      - es-cluster-1.es-cluster
      - es-cluster-2.es-cluster
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - es-cluster-0
      - es-cluster-1
      - es-cluster-2
    node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF
kubectl apply -f /tmp/es-config-bootstrap.yaml
echo "=== restart pods to pick up new config ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch delete pod es-cluster-0 es-cluster-1 es-cluster-2

configmap/es-config configured
=== restart pods to pick up new config ===
pod "es-cluster-0" deleted from elasticsearch namespace
pod "es-cluster-1" deleted from elasticsearch namespace
pod "es-cluster-2" deleted from elasticsearch namespace

input.command:
for t in $(seq 1 60); do
  ready=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=es-cluster --no-headers 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}' | grep -c '1/1')
  echo "attempt $t: ready=$ready"
  if [ "$ready" = "3" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 10
done
kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=es-cluster

content.text: Cluster is **green** with 3 nodes and a master. Now remove the bootstrap-only setting and do a rolling restart to prove safety.

input.command:
cat > /tmp/es-config-final.yaml <<'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${POD_NAME}
    node.roles: [ master, data, ingest ]
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts:
      - es-cluster-0.es-cluster
      - es-cluster-1.es-cluster
      - es-cluster-2.es-cluster
    node.store.allow_mmap: false
EOF
kubectl apply -f /tmp/es-config-final.yaml
echo "=== configmap no longer contains initial_master_nodes ==="
kubectl -n elasticsearch get cm es-config -o jsonpath='{.data.elasticsearch\.yml}' | grep -c initial_master_nodes || true

content.text: Now a rolling restart (one pod at a time, waiting for green between each) to verify the cluster survives restarts without the bootstrap setting.

input.command:
set -e
wait_green() {
  until s=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch exec es-cluster-0 -- curl -s localhost:9200/_cluster/health 2>/dev/null | grep -o '"status":"[a-z]*"'); [ "$s" = '"status":"green"' ] || [ "$s" = '"status":"yellow"' ]; do sleep 5; done
  echo "  health=$s"
}
for p in es-cluster-2 es-cluster-1 es-cluster-0; do
  echo "=== restarting $p ==="
  kubectl -n elasticsearch delete pod $p
  until [ "$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get pod $p --no-headers 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}')" = "1/1" ]; do sleep 5; done
  echo "  $p Ready"
  wait_green
done
echo "=== rolling restart complete ==="
